description_long: This is a rugged, portable Makita-branded jobsite radio designed for use in tough environments such as construction sites or garages. The body is primarily blue with black protective bumpers and features a large, sturdy handle on top for easy carrying. The front panel displays a digital screen, several physical buttons, and a prominent rotary dial for tuning or volume control. Both sides have large, hexagonal speaker grilles, and the left side includes a visible Bluetooth logo, indicating wireless audio capability. The back panel is mostly plain, with a label and some molded details, while the overall construction appears robust and weather-resistant. The object is built from a combination of hard plastic, metal, and likely some rubberized elements for shock absorption.
description: A rugged Makita jobsite radio with a blue and black body, large handle, digital display, and prominent side speakers. Built for durability and portable use in tough environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital display, control buttons, rotary dial, and the robust handle on top.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the Makita logo, Bluetooth symbol, and a large hexagonal speaker grille.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a mostly plain blue panel with a label and the back of the handle.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with another speaker grille and visible handle structure.
